2605.10286.title: AgentRx: A Benchmark Study of LLM Agents for Multimodal Clinical Prediction Tasks
2605.10286.paper_summary: Building effective clinical decision support systems requires the synthesis of complex heterogeneous multimodal data. Such modalities include temporal electronic health records data, medical images, radiology reports, and clinical notes. Large language model (LLM)-based agents have shown impressive performance in various healthcare tasks, especially those involving textual modalities. Considering the fragmentation of healthcare data across hospital systems, collaborative agent frameworks present a promising direction to mitigate data sharing challenges. However, the effectiveness of LLM agents for multimodal clinical risk prediction remains largely unexamined. In this work, we conduct a systematic evaluation of LLM-based agents for clinical prediction tasks using large-scale real-world data. We assess performance in unimodal and multimodal settings and quantify performance gaps between single agent and multi-agent systems. Our findings highlight that single agent frameworks outperform naive multi-agent systems, are better at handling multimodal data, and are better calibrated. This underscores a critical need for improving multi-agent collaboration to better handle heterogeneous inputs. By open-sourcing our code and evaluation framework, this work offers a new benchmark to support future developments relating to agentic systems in healthcare.
2605.10286.paper_summary_zh: 建立有效的臨床決策支持系統需要綜合複雜的異質多模態數據。<lb/>這些模態包括時間性的電子健康記錄數據、醫學影像、放射學報告和臨床筆記。<lb/>基於大型語言模型（LLM）的代理在各種醫療任務中表現出色，特別是在涉及文本模態的任務中。<lb/>考慮到醫療數據在醫院系統中的碎片化，協作代理框架提供了一個有前景的方向，以減輕數據共享的挑戰。<lb/>然而，LLM代理在多模態臨床風險預測中的有效性仍然 largely 未被檢驗。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們對基於LLM的代理在臨床預測任務中的表現進行了系統評估，使用了大規模的現實世界數據。<lb/>我們評估了單模態和多模態設置中的表現，並量化了單一代理和多代理系統之間的性能差距。<lb/>我們的研究結果強調，單一代理框架的表現優於天真的多代理系統，更能有效處理多模態數據，且更具校準性。<lb/>這突顯了改善多代理協作以更好地處理異質輸入的迫切需求。<lb/>通過開源我們的代碼和評估框架，這項工作提供了一個新的基準，以支持未來與醫療保健中代理系統相關的發展。
2605.10286.author: Baraa Al Jorf et.al.
2605.10286.authors: Baraa Al Jorf, Farah E. Shamout
